name: Lithuania
description: Lithuania is an affordable EU destination with WHO-recognized medical programs and a growing tech sector. English-taught programs are widely available at accredited universities.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Usually, the residents of the hall share common showers and kitchen area. Students share their room with one or two others. This is offset by a comparatively low cost of living Expect the rent to be about 50-100 Euros a month.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Student must show complete balance tuition fee + 1 year living expenses minimum Euro 8,000 to Euro 9,000.
tuitionFees.phd: €2,000–€4,000 per year; some funded positions available

requirements: National Visa (Type D) application at Lithuanian embassy

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 20 hours per week during term
workPermit.postStudy: Temporary residence permit for 6–12 months after graduation

languageRequirements.notes: English programs widely available; Lithuanian for local programs

topUniversities.name: Mediation letter – Provided by the university after tuition fees payment

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for Lithuania?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for Lithuania is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in Lithuania?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
